statements.sql: INSERT INTO silver.UnionAllTarget (ProductAlternateKey, EnglishProductName, Segment) SELECT CAST(ProductID AS NVARCHAR(25)), ProductName, 'red' FROM bronze.Product WHERE Color = 'Red' UNION ALL SELECT CAST(ProductID AS NVARCHAR(25)), ProductName, 'other' FROM bronze.Product WHERE Color <> 'Red' OR Color IS NULL

refactor.extracted_sql: SELECT CAST(ProductID AS NVARCHAR(25)) AS ProductAlternateKey, ProductName AS EnglishProductName, 'red' AS Segment FROM bronze.Product WHERE Color = 'Red' UNION ALL SELECT CAST(ProductID AS NVARCHAR(25)) AS ProductAlternateKey, ProductName AS EnglishProductName, 'other' AS Segment FROM bronze.Product WHERE Color <> 'Red' OR Color IS NULL
refactor.refactored_sql: WITH import AS ( SELECT ProductID, ProductName, Color FROM bronze.Product ), logical AS ( SELECT CAST(ProductID AS NVARCHAR(25)) AS ProductAlternateKey, ProductName AS EnglishProductName, 'red' AS Segment FROM import WHERE Color = 'Red' UNION ALL SELECT CAST(ProductID AS NVARCHAR(25)) AS ProductAlternateKey, ProductName AS EnglishProductName, 'other' AS Segment FROM import WHERE Color <> 'Red' OR Color IS NULL ), final AS ( SELECT ProductAlternateKey, EnglishProductName, Segment FROM logical ) SELECT * FROM final

source_tables.summary: Both SQLs use the same source table: bronze.Product

output_columns.summary: Both SQLs return identical columns in the same order: ProductAlternateKey, EnglishProductName, Segment

joins.summary: No joins present in either SQL

filters.summary: Filter conditions are logically equivalent. Both use UNION ALL to split rows into two segments: Color = 'Red' and Color <> 'Red' OR Color IS NULL. The refactored version applies the same filters within the logical CTE but maintains equivalence.

aggregation_grain.summary: No aggregation in either SQL. Both operate at row level with no GROUP BY. The grain is identical: one output row per input row from bronze.Product.
